exercises.title: Simple Pattern Matching
exercises.description: Implement a function that finds and replaces H-X-H patterns with Z gates.
exercises.starterCode:

    Args:
        gates: List of (name, qubit) tuples
    Returns:
        Optimized list with H-X-H replaced by Z
    """

    # TODO: Implement pattern matching
    # Look for consecutive H-X-H on same qubit
    return result

# Test
gates = [('H', 0), ('X', 0), ('H', 0), ('Y', 1)]
print(find_hxh_pattern(gates))  # [('Z', 0), ('Y', 1)]
exercises.solution:
def find_hxh_pattern(gates):
    """
    Find H-X-H patterns and replace with Z.
    
    Args:
        gates: List of (name, qubit) tuples
    Returns:
        Optimized list with H-X-H replaced by Z
    """
    result = []
    i = 0
    
    while i < len(gates):
        # Check for H-X-H pattern
        if (i + 2 < len(gates) and
            gates[i][0] == 'H' and
            gates[i+1][0] == 'X' and
            gates[i+2][0] == 'H' and
            gates[i][1] == gates[i+1][1] == gates[i+2][1]):
            # Replace with Z on same qubit
            result.append(('Z', gates[i][1]))
            i += 3  # Skip all three gates
        else:
            result.append(gates[i])
            i += 1
    
    return result

# Test
gates = [('H', 0), ('X', 0), ('H', 0), ('Y', 1)]
print(find_hxh_pattern(gates))  # [('Z', 0), ('Y', 1)]
hints: Check three consecutive gates at a time
Verify all three gates are on the same qubit
When a match is found, advance by 3 positions

exercises.title: Template Data Structure

        Generate replacement gates with bound qubit values.

            List of (gate_name, actual_qubit) tuples

# Test
t = Template('hxh', [('H', 'q'), ('X', 'q'), ('H', 'q')], [('Z', 'q')])
gates = [('H', 2), ('X', 2), ('H', 2)]
bindings = t.match(gates, 0)
if bindings:
    print(t.apply(bindings))
exercises.solution:
class Template:
    def __init__(self, name, pattern, replacement):
        """
        Create an optimization template.
        
        Args:
            name: Template identifier
            pattern: List of (gate_name, qubit_var) tuples
            replacement: List of (gate_name, qubit_var) tuples
        """
        self.name = name
        self.pattern = pattern
        self.replacement = replacement
    
    def match(self, gates, start):
        """
        Try to match pattern at position start.
        
        Returns:
            Dict mapping qubit variables to actual qubits, or None
        """
        if start + len(self.pattern) > len(gates):
            return None
        
        bindings = {}
        
        for i, (p_name, p_qubit) in enumerate(self.pattern):
            g_name, g_qubit = gates[start + i]
            
            # Gate names must match
            if p_name != g_name:
                return None
            
            # Bind or verify qubit variable
            if p_qubit in bindings:
                if bindings[p_qubit] != g_qubit:
                    return None
            else:
                bindings[p_qubit] = g_qubit
        
        return bindings
    
    def apply(self, bindings):
        """
        Generate replacement gates with bound qubit values.
        
        Returns:
            List of (gate_name, actual_qubit) tuples
        """
        result = []
        for name, qubit_var in self.replacement:
            actual_qubit = bindings[qubit_var]
            result.append((name, actual_qubit))
        return result

# Test
t = Template('hxh', [('H', 'q'), ('X', 'q'), ('H', 'q')], [('Z', 'q')])
gates = [('H', 2), ('X', 2), ('H', 2)]
bindings = t.match(gates, 0)
if bindings:
    print(t.apply(bindings))  # [('Z', 2)]
hints: Use a dictionary to track variable bindings
When encountering a variable, either bind it or verify the existing binding
In apply(), substitute all variables with their bound values

testCases.input: Pattern H-X-H with 'q' variable, gates H(2)-X(2)-H(2)

exercises.title: Template Library Application
exercises.description: Implement a function that applies multiple templates to optimize a circuit.
exercises.starterCode:
def apply_templates(gates, templates):
    """
    Apply a list of templates to optimize gates.
    
    Args:
        gates: List of (name, qubit) tuples
        templates: List of Template objects
    Returns:
        Optimized gate list
    """
    # TODO: Repeatedly scan for matches and apply
    # Continue until no more matches found
    pass

# Templates
class Template:
    def __init__(self, pattern, replacement):
        self.pattern = pattern
        self.replacement = replacement

templates = [
    Template([('H', 'q'), ('H', 'q')], []),  # H-H = I
    Template([('X', 'q'), ('X', 'q')], []),  # X-X = I  
    Template([('T', 'q'), ('T', 'q')], [('S', 'q')]),  # T-T = S
]

gates = [('H', 0), ('T', 0), ('T', 0), ('H', 0)]
print(apply_templates(gates, templates))
exercises.solution:
def apply_templates(gates, templates):
    """
    Apply a list of templates to optimize gates.
    
    Args:
        gates: List of (name, qubit) tuples
        templates: List of Template objects
    Returns:
        Optimized gate list
    """
    gates = list(gates)
    changed = True
    
    while changed:
        changed = False
        i = 0
        
        while i < len(gates):
            matched = False
            
            for template in templates:
                # Try to match this template at position i
                if i + len(template.pattern) > len(gates):
                    continue
                
                bindings = {}
                match = True
                
                for j, (p_name, p_qubit) in enumerate(template.pattern):
                    g_name, g_qubit = gates[i + j]
                    
                    if p_name != g_name:
                        match = False
                        break
                    
                    if p_qubit in bindings:
                        if bindings[p_qubit] != g_qubit:
                            match = False
                            break
                    else:
                        bindings[p_qubit] = g_qubit
                
                if match:
                    # Apply replacement
                    replacement = []
                    for name, qubit_var in template.replacement:
                        replacement.append((name, bindings[qubit_var]))
                    
                    # Replace in gates list
                    gates = gates[:i] + replacement + gates[i + len(template.pattern):]
                    changed = True
                    matched = True
                    break
            
            if not matched:
                i += 1
    
    return gates

# Templates
class Template:
    def __init__(self, pattern, replacement):
        self.pattern = pattern
        self.replacement = replacement

templates = [
    Template([('H', 'q'), ('H', 'q')], []),  # H-H = I
    Template([('X', 'q'), ('X', 'q')], []),  # X-X = I  
    Template([('T', 'q'), ('T', 'q')], [('S', 'q')]),  # T-T = S
]

gates = [('H', 0), ('T', 0), ('T', 0), ('H', 0)]
print(apply_templates(gates, templates))  # [('S', 0)] or similar
hints: Use an outer loop that continues until no changes occur
For each position, try all templates until one matches
When a match is found, replace and restart from that position

# Test

    ('CX', 1, 0),
    ('CX', 0, 1),
    ('H', 2),

exercises.solution:
def find_swap_patterns(gates):
    """
    Find CNOT-CNOT-CNOT patterns that form SWAP.
    
    SWAP pattern: CX(a,b) - CX(b,a) - CX(a,b)
    
    Args:
        gates: List of (name, control, target) tuples for CX
               or (name, qubit) for single-qubit gates
    Returns:
        List of (start_index, (qubit_a, qubit_b)) for each SWAP found
    """
    swaps = []
    i = 0
    
    while i + 2 < len(gates):
        g1, g2, g3 = gates[i], gates[i+1], gates[i+2]
        
        # Check if all three are CX gates
        if (g1[0] == 'CX' and g2[0] == 'CX' and g3[0] == 'CX'):
            # Extract control and target
            c1, t1 = g1[1], g1[2]
            c2, t2 = g2[1], g2[2]
            c3, t3 = g3[1], g3[2]
            
            # Check SWAP pattern: CX(a,b) - CX(b,a) - CX(a,b)
            if (c1 == c3 and t1 == t3 and  # First and third same
                c1 == t2 and t1 == c2):      # Middle is swapped
                swaps.append((i, (c1, t1)))
                i += 3  # Skip past this pattern
                continue
        
        i += 1
    
    return swaps

def replace_cnot_swaps(gates):
    """
    Replace three-CNOT patterns with SWAP gates.
    
    Returns:
        Optimized gate list
    """
    gates = list(gates)
    swaps = find_swap_patterns(gates)
    
    # Replace from end to preserve indices
    for start_idx, (q_a, q_b) in reversed(swaps):
        # Remove three CNOTs, insert SWAP
        gates = gates[:start_idx] + [('SWAP', q_a, q_b)] + gates[start_idx + 3:]
    
    return gates

# Test
gates = [
    ('CX', 0, 1),
    ('CX', 1, 0),
    ('CX', 0, 1),
    ('H', 2),
]
print(replace_cnot_swaps(gates))  # [('SWAP', 0, 1), ('H', 2)]
hints: A SWAP is CX(a,b)-CX(b,a)-CX(a,b) where control and target alternate
Check that the first and third CX have the same control/target
Check that the middle CX has swapped control/target
When replacing, process from end to start to keep indices valid
